claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 15 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and the children in your care.

Let me start with the first question:

**How many children are you inquiring about for Child Benefit, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking to see if I can claim Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex, who is 13, and Blake, who is 15.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have two children - Alex (age 13) and Blake (age 15). 

Before I assess your eligibility, I need to gather a bit more information. Let me start with this:

**Do both Alex and Blake live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me at home, but Blake doesn't - Blake is staying elsewhere.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I need to understand Blake's situation a bit better.

**Where is Blake staying, and who is Blake staying with?** (For example, is Blake with another parent, a relative, in care, or somewhere else?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake is in care at the moment - they're at a local authority care home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful to know.

Now, let me ask about Alex who lives with you:

**Is Alex currently in full-time education, or if not, are they in an approved training scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is only 13, so yes, they're in school full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to check one more important thing about your eligibility:

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status in the UK?** (Child Benefit eligibility depends on your immigration status.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live here in the UK - I'm a British citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent. I now have the information I need to assess your eligibility. Let me review the rules to make sure I give you an accurate answer.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the rules I've reviewed, I have enough information to provide you with my assessment. However, I need one clarification regarding Blake before I give my final decision:

**How long has Blake been in the care home?** (Specifically, is it 8 weeks or less, or longer than 8 weeks?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been about a month - so around 4 weeks or so.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you for that information. I now have everything I need to provide you with my eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary

**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's what this means:

**For Alex:** You qualify for standard Child Benefit as Alex is under 16, lives with you, and is in full-time education.

**For Blake:** You also qualify for Child Benefit even though Blake is in a care home. Since Blake has only been in care for about 4 weeks, this falls within the initial 8-week period where you automatically qualify without needing to make contributions to Blake's upkeep.

**Important note:** If Blake remains in the care home beyond 8 weeks, you would need to contact HMRC (HM Revenue and Customs). At that point, you could continue to claim Child Benefit if you contribute towards Blake's upkeep (such as food, clothes, pocket money, or financial contributions to their accommodation) in an amount equal to or greater than the Child Benefit payment itself.

If you're ready to claim, you can apply through the official government channels. If you have any further questions or if Blake's circumstances change, I'd recommend contacting HMRC directly for guidance.

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (age 13); (2) Alex lives with you; (3) You are a British citizen and live in the UK, meeting the residency and immigration status requirements; (4) As Alex is in full-time education (school), they meet the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Blake is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Blake is under 16 years old (age 15); (2) Although Blake is in a local authority care home and does not live with you, the rules allow for continued eligibility when a child goes into care for more than 8 weeks if you continue to make contributions to their upkeep. Since Blake has only been in care for approximately 4 weeks (less than 8 weeks), you are automatically entitled to Child Benefit for Blake during this initial period without needing to make contributions. (3) You are a British citizen and live in the UK, meeting the residency and immigration status requirements.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake. Alex qualifies as a standard case - living with you and in full-time education. Blake qualifies under the temporary provision for children in care - as Blake has been in the care home for only about 4 weeks, which is within the initial 8-week period, you automatically qualify without needing to make additional contributions. If Blake remains in care beyond 8 weeks, you would need to contact HMRC and potentially make contributions towards Blake's upkeep to continue receiving Child Benefit.

Blake is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Blake is under 16 years old (age 15); (2) Although Blake is in a local authority care home and does not live with you, the rules allow for continued eligibility when a child goes into care for more than 8 weeks if you continue to make contributions to their upkeep. Since Blake has only been in care for approximately 4 weeks (less than 8 weeks), you are automatically entitled to Child Benefit for Blake during this initial period without needing to make contributions. (3) You are a British citizen and live in the UK, meeting the residency and immigration status requirements.

Blake qualifies under the temporary provision for children in care - as Blake has been in the care home for only about 4 weeks, which is within the initial 8-week period, you automatically qualify without needing to make additional contributions. If Blake remains in care beyond 8 weeks, you would need to contact HMRC and potentially make contributions towards Blake's upkeep to continue receiving Child Benefit.
